description_long: This is a vintage-style record player with a rectangular wooden base and a hinged, semi-transparent dust cover. The wood has a rich, dark brown finish, giving the device a classic and elegant appearance. The dust cover is made of a dark, translucent plastic and is shown in an open position, revealing the turntable platter and tonearm inside. The platter is circular and dark, occupying a central position within the wooden enclosure. The tonearm is visible, positioned to the right of the platter, and appears to be made of metal or plastic. There are no visible control knobs or buttons on the exterior surfaces, suggesting a minimalist design or controls located elsewhere.
description: A vintage wooden record player with a dark, translucent dust cover, visible platter, and tonearm inside. The design is classic and minimal, with a rich brown finish and no visible exterior controls.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open, angled dust cover and the dark wooden body, with the platter partially visible inside.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the wooden enclosure and the full width of the open dust cover.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the open dust cover and the platter inside, with the tonearm less visible.
description_view_3: Front view with the dust cover open, clearly displaying the platter and the tonearm inside the wooden enclosure.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and centrally located within the wooden enclosure. It appears to be made of a dark material, likely plastic or metal, and is relatively large compared to the overall size of the record player. The surface is smooth with subtle grooves, typical of a platter designed to hold vinyl records.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, positioned to the right of the platter, and appears to be of medium length with a straight shape. It is likely made of metal or plastic, with a dark finish, and is resting in its holder rather than over the platter.
attribute_dust_cover: The dust cover is present, made of dark, translucent plastic, and is attached to the back of the wooden enclosure with hinges. It is shown in an open position, allowing access to the platter and tonearm, and can likely be closed to protect the interior components.
